---
title: "Packages"
description: "The EpiAware ecosystem. Small, composable packages that combine into infectious disease models."
toc: false
page-layout: full
---

Each package does one thing and combines with the others.
Most extend Julia's [Distributions.jl](https://github.com/JuliaStats/Distributions.jl), so they share a common interface and compose cleanly.
Package names link to their documentation, and the icon beside each links to its source.

Status reflects where a package sits in its release cycle rather than a judgement of quality.
[experimental]{.status-pill .status-experimental} is usable but not yet a registered release, so expect the API to change.
[maturing]{.status-pill .status-maturing} is registered and installable, on a `0.x` line while the API settles.
[stable]{.status-pill .status-stable} is a `1.0`+ release with a committed, semantically versioned API, and nothing is there yet.

```{=html}
<div class="filter-table-wrap">
<div class="filter-controls">
  <input type="search" class="filter-search"
         placeholder="Search packages" aria-label="Search packages">
  <div class="filter-buttons" role="group" aria-label="Filter packages by category">
    <button class="filter-btn is-active" data-category="all">All</button>
    <button class="filter-btn" data-category="modelling">Modelling</button>
    <button class="filter-btn" data-category="distributions">Distributions</button>
    <button class="filter-btn" data-category="infrastructure">Infrastructure</button>
  </div>
</div>

<table class="data-table">
  <thead>
    <tr><th scope="col">Package</th><th scope="col">What it does</th></tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr data-category="modelling">
      <td class="data-name">
        <a href="https://composableturingidmodels.epiaware.org/dev/">ComposableTuringIDModels.jl</a>
        <a class="data-gh" href="https://github.com/EpiAware/ComposableTuringIDModels.jl" aria-label="ComposableTuringIDModels.jl on GitHub"><i class="bi bi-github"></i></a>
        <span class="status-pill status-experimental">experimental</span>
      </td>
      <td>Assemble infectious disease models from interchangeable infection and observation parts as a single Turing model. The package that ties the ecosystem together.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr data-category="distributions">
      <td class="data-name">
        <a href="https://censoreddistributions.epiaware.org/">CensoredDistributions.jl</a>
        <a class="data-gh" href="https://github.com/EpiAware/CensoredDistributions.jl" aria-label="CensoredDistributions.jl on GitHub"><i class="bi bi-github"></i></a>
        <span class="status-pill status-maturing">maturing</span>
      </td>
      <td>Handle the interval censoring and truncation biases common in epidemiological delay data, wrapping any Distributions.jl distribution.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr data-category="distributions">
      <td class="data-name">
        <a href="https://composeddistributions.epiaware.org/dev/">ComposedDistributions.jl</a>
        <a class="data-gh" href="https://github.com/EpiAware/ComposedDistributions.jl" aria-label="ComposedDistributions.jl on GitHub"><i class="bi bi-github"></i></a>
        <span class="status-pill status-experimental">experimental</span>
      </td>
      <td>Join the delays in a case's history into a single distribution that can simulate a case or be fitted to observed ones.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr data-category="distributions">
      <td class="data-name">
        <a href="https://convolveddistributions.epiaware.org/dev/">ConvolvedDistributions.jl</a>
        <a class="data-gh" href="https://github.com/EpiAware/ConvolvedDistributions.jl" aria-label="ConvolvedDistributions.jl on GitHub"><i class="bi bi-github"></i></a>
        <span class="status-pill status-maturing">maturing</span>
      </td>
      <td>Convolve probability distributions, with shared numerical quadrature.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr data-category="distributions">
      <td class="data-name">
        <a href="https://modifieddistributions.epiaware.org/dev/">ModifiedDistributions.jl</a>
        <a class="data-gh" href="https://github.com/EpiAware/ModifiedDistributions.jl" aria-label="ModifiedDistributions.jl on GitHub"><i class="bi bi-github"></i></a>
        <span class="status-pill status-experimental">experimental</span>
      </td>
      <td>Rescale, weight, modify hazards, or transform a distribution, while keeping the full Distributions.jl interface.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr data-category="distributions">
      <td class="data-name">
        <a href="https://lowereddistributions.epiaware.org/dev/">LoweredDistributions.jl</a>
        <a class="data-gh" href="https://github.com/EpiAware/LoweredDistributions.jl" aria-label="LoweredDistributions.jl on GitHub"><i class="bi bi-github"></i></a>
        <span class="status-pill status-experimental">experimental</span>
      </td>
      <td>Lower a distribution, or a whole composed event tree, onto a backend-agnostic dynamical-systems representation (a phase-type chain or a CTMC), then hand it to Catalyst, SciML, AlgebraicPetri, or JumpProcesses.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr data-category="distributions">
      <td class="data-name">
        <a href="https://github.com/EpiAware/DistributionsInference.jl">DistributionsInference.jl</a>
        <a class="data-gh" href="https://github.com/EpiAware/DistributionsInference.jl" aria-label="DistributionsInference.jl on GitHub"><i class="bi bi-github"></i></a>
        <span class="status-pill status-experimental">experimental</span>
      </td>
      <td>A PPL-neutral fit protocol and log-density engine: any object that names its own parameters becomes fittable, with Turing.jl, Bijectors, and FlexiChains readback layered on as extensions rather than requirements.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr data-category="infrastructure">
      <td class="data-name">
        <a href="https://epiawarepackagetools.epiaware.org/dev/">EpiAwarePackageTools.jl</a>
        <a class="data-gh" href="https://github.com/EpiAware/EpiAwarePackageTools.jl" aria-label="EpiAwarePackageTools.jl on GitHub"><i class="bi bi-github"></i></a>
        <span class="status-pill status-experimental">experimental</span>
      </td>
      <td>Shared scaffolding, quality checks, and test utilities used across the ecosystem.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr data-category="infrastructure">
      <td class="data-name">
        <a href="https://epiawareadtools.epiaware.org/dev/">EpiAwareADTools.jl</a>
        <a class="data-gh" href="https://github.com/EpiAware/EpiAwareADTools.jl" aria-label="EpiAwareADTools.jl on GitHub"><i class="bi bi-github"></i></a>
        <span class="status-pill status-maturing">maturing</span>
      </td>
      <td>Automatic differentiation safety machinery for the stack, tested across ForwardDiff, ReverseDiff, Mooncake, and Enzyme. Deliberately framed as fixes we host while we try to fix things upstream.</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

<p class="filter-empty" hidden>No packages match that search.</p>
</div>
```

{{< include ../_includes/filter-table.qmd >}}

## Related tools {#related-tools}

Other Julia efforts working towards composable, reusable infectious disease
modelling, built outside EpiAware.
We think interoperability matters more than where a tool is built, so we link to
them and aim to work well alongside them.

| Tool | What it does |
|---|---|
| [gemlib](https://gem-epidemics.gitlab.io/gemlib/) | Building blocks for assembling epidemic models and fitting them, in Python. It puts particular weight on composable inference, through JAX, TensorFlow Probability, and specialised samplers. That matters to us too, though our first concern is composing the model itself and supporting the workflow around it. |
| [EpiBranch.jl](https://epiforecasts.io/EpiBranch.jl/dev/) ([source](https://github.com/epiforecasts/EpiBranch.jl)) | Branching process models for infectious disease epidemiology. |
| [DDSALineLists.jl](https://github.com/epirecipes/DDSALineLists.jl) | Synthetic epidemic line lists from a discrete-time dynamic survival analysis generative model. |
| [CategoricalOutbreaks.jl](https://github.com/epirecipes/CategoricalOutbreaks.jl) | Categorical outbreak line-list representations and aggregation, built with Catlab. |

Building something in this space?
[Tell us on the forum](https://community.epinowcast.org/) and we will add it.
